# Career framework — skills × leadership

A two-axis career and remuneration framework, implementing the Corporate Rebels
"remuneration diamond" for flat organisations. Live at https://2d-career.coscient.workers.dev

Source of the method:
https://www.corporate-rebels.com/blog/remuneration-method-for-flat-organizations

## The model

Two axes, three levels each, giving nine grades written as `<leadership><skill>` — 1A … 3C.

- Skills & capabilities: A foundational, B developed, C advanced. Stored as 1 | 2 | 3.
- Leadership: 1 self, 2 team, 3 organisation. Scope of responsibility, not headcount.

Each grade maps to a pay band; several grades share a band on purpose, so a deep specialist
(1C) can be paid like a team lead (2B).

Peers score the colleagues they work with. Each assessment averages its own items into one
skill number and one leadership number; those are averaged across peers, then rounded to a
grade. Self-assessments are recorded and displayed but never counted. The result is advice
to whoever owns pay, never an automatic decision.

## Two workspaces

- Local: no account, state in browser localStorage, nothing uploaded.
- Team: Google sign-in, join by 6-character code, state in Cloudflare R2, shared by the team.
  Scores are attributed to the signed-in account and visible to every member.

## Data model

```
AppState  { currency, tracks[], leadership[], people[], assessments[], bands[] }
Track     { id, name, summary, capabilities: [{ id, name, levels: {1,2,3} }] }
Leadership{ id, name, levels: {1,2,3} }            # shared by all tracks
Person    { id, name, role, trackId, accountId? }  # accountId = claimed by an account
Assessment{ id, personId, rater, raterId?, kind: 'self'|'peer', date,
            skills: {capabilityId: 1|2|3}, leadership: {dimensionId: 1|2|3}, note }
PayBand   { id, label, grades: ['1C','2B','3A'], amount }
```

`levels` values are always 1 | 2 | 3; on the skills axis they read as A | B | C.
`currency` is free text — a symbol, a code, or empty for bare numbers.

## Import / export

The UI exports and imports this shape as JSON. `/api` is not needed for that: download a
starting file from the Template button, fill it in, and import it. Minimum viable file
requires `tracks` and `bands`; `people` and `assessments` may be empty.

## HTTP API

Two credentials work: the browser session cookie from Google sign-in, or a personal access
token. A signed-in user mints a token on the Team tab; it is shown once and can be revoked
there at any time.

```
curl -H "Authorization: Bearer car_xxx_yyy" https://2d-career.coscient.workers.dev/api/me
```

A token acts as the person who minted it: same teams, same role, and their name recorded as
the rater on anything it submits. Token management (`/api/tokens*`) is deliberately
cookie-only, so a leaked token cannot mint more tokens or revoke existing ones.

```
GET    /api/me                                   { user, teams, signInEnabled }
POST   /api/teams                                { name }
POST   /api/teams/join                           { code }
GET    /api/teams/:id                            { team, state }
PUT    /api/teams/:id                            { state, version }   409 if stale
PATCH  /api/teams/:id                            { name }             admin
DELETE /api/teams/:id                                                 owner, irreversible
POST   /api/teams/:id/assessments                { assessment }
DELETE /api/teams/:id/assessments/:assessmentId                       rater or admin
POST   /api/teams/:id/claim                      { personId | null }
PUT    /api/teams/:id/members/:userId            { role }             owner
DELETE /api/teams/:id/members/:userId                                 admin
POST   /api/teams/:id/code                                            admin
POST   /api/teams/:id/leave

DELETE /api/me                                                        cookie only, deletes account
GET    /api/tokens                               { tokens }           cookie only
POST   /api/tokens                               { name } → { token, secret }  cookie only
DELETE /api/tokens/:id                                                cookie only
```

Typical agent flow: `GET /api/me` to find the team id, `GET /api/teams/:id` for the
framework, roster and scores, then `POST /api/teams/:id/assessments` to submit a score.
Framework edits use `PUT /api/teams/:id` with the `stateVersion` from the last read; on 409
re-read and retry rather than forcing.

Roles: member < admin < owner. Exactly one owner per team. Every rule is enforced
server-side, not only in the UI.

## Lifecycle

Deleting an account removes its tokens and memberships, strips its name from scores it gave
(the scores stay, so grades do not move), and passes owned teams to another member.

Teams are isolated — separate roster, framework, bands and scores; one account gets a
separate roster row per team. Removing a member keeps their row and their scores. Deleting a
team destroys its framework, roster and scores permanently; there is no undo.

## Working on the code

See AGENTS.md in the repository for architecture, conventions and constraints.
Styled with the Keel design system (https://keel.coscient.workers.dev).
